content.full_text:
A-118-483

The Superior Court
1725 MAIN STREET
SANTA MONICA, CALIFORNIA 90401
CHARLES A. LORING JUDGE

TELEPHONE
(213) 870-0131

July 17, 1978

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

Recently I received a communication from you stating that
all candidates for political office, including judges, on the June
6 primary ballot were required to file a post-election campaign
statement no later than Monday, July 31. However, we have information
indirectly from your Los Angeles office that this requirement does
not apply to judges whose re-election on the June 6, 1978, primary
ballot was uncontested and unopposed, and that such judges need not
file a financial statement until January 1979.

In the event that the last referred to information is
incorrect for any reason, please be advised that the only expenditure
which I incurred in connection with the June 6, 1978, primary election
was the legally required filing fee of $491.66, which fee I paid from
my own pocket. I have not received any contributions, directly or
indirectly, from any source but my own pocket. If a statement is
required from me on or before July 31, 1978, please accept the
foregoing as such statement. If it is not required until January
1979 I will fill out the usual form at that time.

If the above does not satisfy the requirements of the
law, will you please advise me.

Very truly yours,

Charles A. Loring

CAL:ah

Called July 24, 1978 and informed Judge Loring that he has to file
a statement no later than January 31, 1979. Sent Form 430 for his
use.

mak

430

CHAMBERS OF

The Superior Court
1725 MAIN STREET
SANTA MONICA, CALIFORNIA 90401                          TELEPHONE
CHARLES A. LORING, JUDGE                                (213) 870-0131

July 17, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

     Recently I received a communication from you stating that
all candidates for political office, including judges, on the June
6 primary ballot were required to file a post-election campaign
statement no later than Monday July 31. However, we have information
indirectly from your Los Angeles office that this requirement does
not apply to judges whose election on the June 6, 1978 primary
ballot was uncontested and unopposed, and that such judges need not
file a financial statement until January 1979.

     In the event that the last referred to information is
incorrect for any reason, please be advised that the only expenditure
which I incurred in connection with the June 6, 1978 primary election
was the legally required filing fee of $491.66, which fee I paid from
my own pocket. I have not received any contributions, directly or
indirectly, from any source but my own pocket. If a statement is
required from me on or before July 31, 1978, please accept the
foregoing as such statement. If it is not required until January
1979 I will fill out the usual form at that time.

     If the above does not satisfy the requirements of the
law, will you please advise me.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Charles A. Loring
CAL:ah

sections.question: Recently I received a communication from you stating that all candidates for political office, including judges, on the June 6 primary ballot were required to file a post-election campaign statement no later than Monday July 31. However, we have information indirectly from your Los Angeles office that this requirement does not apply to judges whose reelection on the June 6, 1978, primary ballot was uncontested and unopposed, and that such judges need not file a financial statement until January 1979. If the above does not satisfy the requirements of the law, will you please advise me.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a judge whose reelection on the June 6, 1978 primary ballot was uncontested need to file a post-election campaign statement by July 31, 1978, or is the deadline January 1979?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a judge seeking advice. The document includes a handwritten FPPC staff note indicating they called the requestor with guidance, but this note is not a formal written response. The duplicate letter text suggests this may be a filing copy. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present.

embedding.summary: Judge Charles A. Loring seeks clarification on whether unopposed judges in the June 6, 1978 primary election must file post-election campaign statements by July 31, 1978 or January 1979, and submits his expenditure information conditionally. The FPPC's handwritten note indicates Loring was called on July 24, 1978 and informed the filing deadline is January 31, 1979.
